steps.title: Reverse the relationship between populations
steps.content:
Given that the 2020 population is 1.32 times the 2016 population, I need to reverse this to express 2016 in terms of 2020.

If $Z_{2020} = 1.32 \cdot Z_{2016}$, then dividing both sides by 1.32:
$$Z_{2016} = \frac{1}{1.32} \cdot Z_{2020}$$

steps.title: Set up the percentage equation
steps.content:
The problem asks for $p$ such that 2016 is $p\%$ of 2020:
$$Z_{2016} = \frac{p}{100} \cdot Z_{2020}$$
Comparing with the equation from Step 1, the coefficient of $Z_{2020}$ must match:
$$\frac{p}{100} = \frac{1}{1.32}$$

steps.title: Solve for p and round to nearest integer
steps.content:
Multiplying both sides by 100:
$$p = \frac{100}{1.32}$$
Calculating: $100 \div 1.32 \approx 75.76$

Rounding to the nearest whole number, $p = 76$.
